question: 核查R004239和R004240是否构成重复报销、拆分报销或业务招待费超标准
policy_questions: What are the per-person limits for business entertainment by employee level?
What constitutes duplicate reimbursement under the 2025 policy?
What constitutes split reimbursement under the 2025 policy?
Are there any exemptions or special approval thresholds for business entertainment?
data_questions: Are R004239 and R004240 linked to distinct invoices and vendors?
Are there other records from E0008 around the same dates that could indicate splitting?
What is the per-person amount for each record (amount/participants)?
Do the invoices show overlapping or identical content?
Is there any pattern of consecutive-day same-vendor claims from E0008?
evidence_requirements: Full details of R004239 and R004240
Policy excerpts for business entertainment standards
Policy excerpts for duplicate and split reimbursement definitions
Related records from E0008 in October 2025
Invoice details for INV004233 and INV004234

reasons: Multi-rule check: duplicate, split, and entertainment standard (3 rules)
Requires policy research across 2 policies (reimbursement + entertainment)
Needs batch analysis for related record checks and pattern detection
Clean-but-suspicious trap type requiring careful no-anomaly determination

subagent_plan.task_description: Research applicable business entertainment standards, per-person limits by employee level, and definitions of duplicate/split reimbursement in the 2025 reimbursement policy

stopping_conditions: All three rule questions answered with policy citations
Evidence matrix shows 100% coverage with pass status
